---
title: "Functional Gene Enrichment Report"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: '2'
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

## 1. Load Required Libraries

```{r libraries}
packages <- c(
  "readxl"
  ,"clusterProfiler"
  ,"org.Hs.eg.db"
  ,"org.Ce.eg.db"
  ,"enrichplot"
  ,"ReactomePA"
  ,"ggplot2"
  ,"dplyr"
  ,"ggplot2"
  ,"forcats"
  ,"dplyr")

# Install only what's missing (BiocManager handles both CRAN and Bioconductor)
missing_pkgs <- packages[!sapply(packages, requireNamespace, quietly = TRUE)]
if (length(missing_pkgs) > 0) {
  BiocManager::install(missing_pkgs)
}

# Load all
invisible(lapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE))

```

## 2. Input Gene Lists
### 2.1 Worm Genes

```{r load-worm}
# Table 1 is inside the file "List of verified genes.xlsx" as input of the enrichment analysis here
file_path_worm <- "./06-2024/List of verified genes.xlsx" 
worm_df_old <- read_excel(file_path_worm)

file_path_worm <- "./05-2026/list of genes_resubmission.xlsx" 
worm_df <- read_excel(file_path_worm)

wb_col <- grep("WormBase Gene", colnames(worm_df), ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE)
worm_df <- worm_df %>% rename(GeneID = all_of(wb_col))
worm_map <- bitr(worm_df$GeneID, fromType = "WORMBASE", toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb = org.Ce.eg.db)
worm_entrez <- unique(worm_map$ENTREZID)
```

```{r}

# all mapped Entrez IDs
mapped_entrez <- unique(worm_map$ENTREZID)

# Check GO annotations for those Entrez IDs
bp_annot <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  org.Ce.eg.db,
  keys    = mapped_entrez,
  keytype = "ENTREZID",
  columns = c("GO", "ONTOLOGY")
)

# Keep only BP ontology
bp_annot <- bp_annot %>% 
  filter(ONTOLOGY == "BP" & !is.na(GO))

# Entrez IDs that have at least one BP GO term
genes_with_bp <- unique(bp_annot$ENTREZID)

length(genes_with_bp)
# should be 26

# Which 4 mapped genes have NO BP annotation?
no_bp_entrez <- setdiff(mapped_entrez, genes_with_bp)
no_bp_entrez
```



### 3.4 C. elegans GO: Biological Process

```{r worm-go-bp}
# Note: the gene list (n = 18) is too small for BH-corrected enrichment to
# survive multiple testing (~1,963 BP terms). We therefore use no p-value
# adjustment and report nominal Fisher's exact p < 0.05. This should be
# disclosed in the methods section of the manuscript.
ego_ce_bp <- enrichGO(gene = worm_entrez, OrgDb = org.Ce.eg.db, keyType = "ENTREZID",
                      ont = "BP", pAdjustMethod = "none", pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
                      readable = TRUE)
cat("BP enriched terms (nominal p < 0.05):", nrow(ego_ce_bp@result), "\n")
# dotplot(ego_ce_bp, showCategory = 20) + ggtitle("C. elegans GO Enrichment (Biological Process)")
```
```{r}
# (Optional) Manually define a custom display order for the y-axis.
# Set to NULL to use top-N terms ordered automatically by p-value.
custom_order <- NULL
# Example:
# custom_order <- c(
#   "Golgi to plasma membrane transport",
#   "post-Golgi vesicle-mediated transport",
#   "regulation of protein localization",
#   "regulation of localization",
#   "vesicle-mediated transport to the plasma membrane",
#   "regulation of vesicle-mediated transport"
# )

# How many top terms to show when custom_order is NULL
top_n_bp <- 10

# Step 1: Build plot_data
if (!is.null(custom_order)) {
  plot_data <- ego_ce_bp@result %>%
    dplyr::filter(Description %in% custom_order) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      log10_p  = -log10(pvalue),
      Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(custom_order))
    )
} else {
  plot_data <- ego_ce_bp@result %>%
    dplyr::arrange(pvalue) %>%
    dplyr::slice(1:top_n_bp) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(pvalue)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      log10_p     = -log10(pvalue),
      Description = factor(Description, levels = Description)
    )
}

# Step 2: Plot
dotplot_2nd <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = log10_p, y = Description)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = Count, color = GeneRatio), alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_size(range = c(3, 8)) +
  labs(
    x = expression(-log[10]~italic(p)),
    y = "GO Term",
    title = "C. elegans GO Enrichment (Biological Process)",
    caption = "Nominal p-value (no multiple-testing correction); n = 18 genes"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title  = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.8)
  )
dotplot_2nd
# One can find the final output of dotplot in manuscript figure 2 panel a
```

```{r}
ggsave("./output_plots/dotplot_2nd_C.e.Biological_Process.pdf", dotplot_2nd, dpi = 600)
```

```{r worm-go-cc}
# Same rationale as BP: no adjustment due to small gene list
ego_ce_cc <- enrichGO(gene = worm_entrez, OrgDb = org.Ce.eg.db, keyType = "ENTREZID",
                      ont = "CC", pAdjustMethod = "none", pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
                      readable = TRUE)
cat("CC enriched terms (nominal p < 0.05):", nrow(ego_ce_cc@result), "\n")
# dotplot(ego_ce_cc, showCategory = 20) + ggtitle("C. elegans 
# GO Enrichment (Cellular Component)")
```

```{r}
# How many top CC terms to display
top_n_cc <- 10

plot_data_cc <- ego_ce_cc@result %>%
  dplyr::arrange(pvalue) %>%
  dplyr::slice(1:top_n_cc) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(pvalue)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    log10_p     = -log10(pvalue),
    Description = factor(Description, levels = Description)
  )

dotplotCC_2nd <- ggplot(plot_data_cc, aes(x = log10_p, y = Description)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = Count, color = GeneRatio), alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_size(range = c(3, 8)) +
  labs(
    x = expression(-log[10]~italic(p)),
    y = "GO Term",
    title = "C. elegans GO Enrichment (Cellular Component)",
    caption = "Nominal p-value (no multiple-testing correction); n = 18 genes"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title  = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.8),
    plot.margin  = margin(15, 15, 15, 15)
  )
dotplotCC_2nd
# One can find the final output of dotplot in manuscript figure 2 panel b
```

```{r}
ggsave("./output_plots/dotplot_2nd_C.e.Cellular_Compartment.pdf", dotplotCC_2nd, dpi = 600)
```
## 4. Export Results (Optional)

```{r export-results}
# write.csv(as.data.frame(ego_bp), "Human_GO_BP.csv", row.names = FALSE)
# write.csv(as.data.frame(ego_mf), "Human_GO_MF.csv", row.names = FALSE)
# write.csv(as.data.frame(ereactome), "Human_Reactome.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(as.data.frame(ego_ce_bp), "Worm_GO_BP.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
